title: decider self-apply close-issues step: append closure-ledger.jsonl entry alongside the closed-issues.json write; backfill 12 missing entries from 2026-05-18..2026-05-24
related_findings: FND-04 in monitor/tinker/report-2026-05-25T02-48.json (PROP-030 net_velocity_7d falsely-low because ledger is the metric source)

executive_summary.problem_one_line: When the decider self-apply patch path closes an issue (moves it from open-issues.json to closed-issues.json with status='fixed-pending-verification' or 'closed'), it writes ONLY to closed-issues.json. It does NOT also append to closure-ledger.jsonl. The M1 sweep path, the BAU triage path, and the M3 carry-over self-test all write ledger lines; the self-apply close path does not.
executive_summary.fix_one_line: Add a fs.appendFileSync('monitor/decisions/closure-ledger.jsonl', ...) call inside the per-issue close loop in monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md Step 1 — emitting one ledger line per closed ISS with closed_by_mechanism='self-apply' and action_taken='patch'. Plus a one-time backfill script for the 12 missing entries from 2026-05-18 through 2026-05-25.
executive_summary.blast_radius_one_line: Low: a 5-line addition to one bash node block. Backfill is mechanical (read closed-issues.json, filter the 12 missing ids, append synthesized ledger lines preserving original closed_at).

problem_detail.code_location: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md, Step 1 (line ~588-617). The bash block reads open-issues.json and closed-issues.json, moves matching ISSs from open to closed with status='fixed-pending-verification', and writes both files back. There is no fs.appendFileSync to closure-ledger.jsonl anywhere in this block.

comparison_to_other_close_paths.M1_sweep: monitor/prompts/decider.md line 280-294 writes ledger line BEFORE flipping status. Mechanism='M1'. Working correctly — last 5 ledger entries include ISS-2083/2084/2085 from M1 path on 2026-05-17.
comparison_to_other_close_paths.BAU_triage: monitor/prompts/decider.md line 195 self-test enforces every status='open' ≥12h item must have a ledger line with closed_by_mechanism='BAU'. Working correctly — ISS-2189 BAU entry from 2026-05-24T17-20 is in the ledger.
comparison_to_other_close_paths.M3_carry_over: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md line 255-287 self-test enforces every unresolved_prior_cycle entry has a ledger line with closed_by_mechanism='M3'. Working correctly when fired.
comparison_to_other_close_paths.self_apply: monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md line 588-617 (THIS BUG): writes closed-issues.json only, no ledger append. Mechanism intended would be 'self-apply' but it never gets recorded.

direct_evidence.ledger_query_last_7d: node script run 2026-05-25T06:09:46Z: 1 ledger entry in last 7d (ISS-2189). 12 closed-issues.json entries in last 7d. Gap = 11. (ISS-2104 also missing — that one is a 2026-05-18 FUSE-sync-gap closure, separate path, also bypasses ledger.)
direct_evidence.commit_evidence: Commit 5ab70a1 (2026-05-24T09:24Z) 'Decider: patch WIN-019 (4 holes), WIN-009 (1 hole), PRED-073 (5 holes), fix build drift' touched closed-issues.json (11 new entries) but did NOT touch closure-ledger.jsonl. The next ledger-touching commit is 2 days later (the ISS-2189 BAU closure).
direct_evidence.pre_regression_baseline: ISS-2083, ISS-2084, ISS-2085 (closed 2026-05-17T20:20Z by run decider-2026-05-17T20-14Z, closed_by_mechanism='self-apply', action_taken='patch') ARE in the ledger. Same self-apply path, one week before the regression. This proves an earlier version of the close-issues block (or its predecessor M1-path corrective line) was writing the ledger and the 2026-05-24 LLM either improvised a different close script or the documentation no longer reflects what works.

downstream_impact.PROP_030_net_velocity_7d: Current run reads net=-12 (moderate tier fires) because closed_issues_velocity_7d sources from the ledger. True net_velocity_7d_truth (from closed-issues.json) is -2 (would be info tier at most). Threshold tier escalation is artifactual.
downstream_impact.PROP_026_burndown_rollback: build-scripts/revert-burndown-closures.js reads ledger entries by run_id/iss/since to revert. If the operator needed to revert one of the 12 missing closures, it cannot be done via the script — would require hand-patching closed-issues.json and open-issues.json. (Low practical risk — these are minor/moderate patches, unlikely to need rollback, but the audit trail invariant is broken.)
downstream_impact.tinker_mode_1_audit: Every Mode 1 audit going forward will see falsely-low closure velocity until ledger and closed-issues.json reconcile. The PROP-030 history table is now contaminated for runs 2026-05-21, -24, -25.

proposed_fix.summary: Two changes: (1) add a per-issue ledger-append inside the existing close-issues node block in decider-patches-and-selfapply.md Step 1; (2) one-time backfill script for the 12 missing entries.

files_to_modify.explanation: Adds a per-issue ledger-append inside the existing close loop. Captures prior_status before mutation, builds a self-apply mechanism ledger line, appends synchronously. Same shape as M1 sweep ledger writes in monitor/prompts/decider.md line 282-294.

files_to_create.purpose: One-time backfill of the 12 missing ledger entries. Idempotent (checks existingIds set). Preserves original closed_at so PROP-030 metrics tally to the right 7-day window. Marks closed_by_mechanism='self-apply-backfill' so audit grep can distinguish retro-writes from canonical ones.

tradeoffs.what_we_gain: PROP-030 net_velocity metric accurate. PROP-026 burndown rollback works for self-apply closures. Audit trail invariant (every closure has a ledger entry) restored. Tinker Mode 1 no longer reports false-low closure velocity.
tradeoffs.what_we_might_lose: Five extra lines per self-apply close path. Marginally larger ledger file (closure-ledger.jsonl is ~50 KB today; grows by one line per closure, no compaction concern at current cadence). No semantic risk to the close-on-success state machine because the ledger-append is additive — failure of fs.appendFileSync would throw and abort the run, which is the correct fail-loud behavior; partial writes are not possible since each line is atomic in append mode.

edge_cases.RUN_ID_not_set: Defaults to 'decider-unknown' in the ledger line. Acceptable — the audit grep still finds the ISS by iss_id, just loses the run-id correlation. Decider already requires RUN_ID for M1 and M3 self-tests, so the env var being absent here would be a pre-existing decider configuration bug not caused by this PROP.
edge_cases.duplicate_closes_same_run: If for some reason the same ISS is closed twice in one run, two ledger lines would result. PROP-026 revert script handles duplicates by treating the latest (or first, depending on flag) — not a correctness concern.
edge_cases.ledger_corruption_mid_append: fs.appendFileSync is atomic per line on POSIX filesystems. A crash between two iterations of the forEach loop would leave a complete prior line and no partial line. The N+1 close lines in closed-issues.json without their N+1 ledger lines would be the worst case — same status quo as today, recoverable by the backfill script using the same TARGET_IDS pattern.

verification_pattern: grep -q 'PROP-055: append closure-ledger line' monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md && test -f build-scripts/backfill-closure-ledger-2026-05.js && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN
verification_pattern_post_backfill: node -e "const fs=require('fs');const ledger=fs.readFileSync('monitor/decisions/closure-ledger.jsonl','utf8').split('\n').filter(Boolean);const ids=new Set(ledger.map(l=>{try{return JSON.parse(l).iss_id}catch{return null}}));const targets=['ISS-2104','ISS-2176','ISS-2177','ISS-2178','ISS-2179','ISS-2180','ISS-2181','ISS-2182','ISS-2183','ISS-2184','ISS-2185','ISS-2186'];const missing=targets.filter(t=>!ids.has(t));console.log(missing.length===0?'FIXED':'STILL_BROKEN: missing '+missing.join(','));"
why_human_needed: Two questions for the operator: (a) should the backfill use closed_by_mechanism='self-apply' (cleaner) or 'self-apply-backfill' (transparent that this is retro-written)? PROP defaults to the latter for audit honesty. (b) Should the ISS-2104 entry (which is an operator-direct FUSE-restore closure, not a decider self-apply) be backfilled under the same mechanism or a distinct 'operator-direct-restore' mechanism enum? PROP defaults to 'self-apply-backfill' for both (the backfill marker carries the distinction in the closure_evidence.description_excerpt). If you prefer a richer enum, edit the backfill script's mechanism field before applying.

estimated_impact.tokens: ~0 — prompt edit adds 14 lines (~250 tokens), but decider doesn't read this file every run, only when patches need to land.
estimated_impact.metric_correctness: Restores PROP-030 net_velocity accuracy from currently-depressed-by-83% to truthful. The current run's net=-12 would have been net=-1 with the fix in place this cycle.
estimated_impact.risk_reduction: Audit trail completeness restored. Any operator-driven rollback of self-apply closures becomes possible again (currently impossible for the 12 missing entries — need hand-patching of closed-issues.json directly).

rollout_plan: 1. Operator (or future tinker Mode 4 run with self-apply empowerment) edits monitor/prompts/reference/decider-patches-and-selfapply.md per files_to_modify above.
2. Operator (or tinker) creates build-scripts/backfill-closure-ledger-2026-05.js with the content above.
3. Run `node build-scripts/backfill-closure-ledger-2026-05.js --dry-run` from a clean clone. Confirm 12 'WOULD APPEND' lines.
5. Commit + push from clean clone with message: 'PROP-055 apply: ledger-append in decider self-apply + backfill 12 entries from 2026-05-18..05-24'.
6. Verify next decider self-apply run writes a fresh ledger line. Verify next tinker run's PROP-030 net_velocity_7d reads close to net_velocity_7d_truth.
7. Update this PROP's status to 'implemented' once steps 1-6 land.
8. The backfill script can be archived or kept for future reference; it is idempotent and safe to re-run.
scope_authority: Tinker authors PROP. Operator applies (consistent with PROP-051/PROP-054 pattern of tinker-proposes / operator-applies for decider-prompt edits).
implementation_note: Applied 2026-05-25T07:12Z by operator via push-via-api.js (Devilwench identity). Both verification patterns return FIXED. Prompt edit adds the PROP-055 ledger-append block in decider-patches-and-selfapply.md close loop; backfill script wrote 12 missing entries (ISS-2104 + ISS-2176..2186) to closure-ledger.jsonl with closed_by_mechanism=self-apply-backfill.
